# Add Explicit Breaking Release Rule to Semantic-Release

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-07 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

The CMA v12 / Node < 22 breaking change (commit `6707b2b`) was released as `v10.1.7` (a patch) instead of `v11.0.0` because the semantic-release configuration lacked a rule mapping `breaking: true` to a major release. The `commit-analyzer` plugin requires an explicit rule entry for this — it does not infer major from `BREAKING CHANGE` footers by default with custom `releaseRules`.

## Decision

Add `{ "breaking": true, "release": "major" }` to the `releaseRules` array in `package.json`'s semantic-release config. A corrective breaking commit was pushed to trigger the proper `v11.0.0` release.

Source: commit `49cdace` — "feat!: add breaking release rule and trigger v11.0.0 [DX-787]"

## Consequences

- Future breaking changes will correctly trigger major version bumps
- The `v10.1.7` release contained breaking changes — consumers who pinned `^10.x` received a breaking update before the corrective `v11.0.0` was published
- The `build(deps)` scope rule (patch) remains, ensuring dependency bumps trigger releases without manual version management
